q: ___ means keeping and protecting a living and changing environment.
___ intervention is required to restore areas and to protect native species.
The ___ reason for conservation is that useful human products like food, fibres, drugs and medicines are obtained from biodiversity.
The ___ reason for conservation is that biodiversity provides ecosystem services like providing oxygen.
The ___ reason for conservation states that every living species has an intrinsic value.
There are mainly two conservation methods; ___ and ex situ conservation methods.
___ methods are the ways of conservation of species which take place in the natural habitat of the organism.
Maintaining the natural habitat means that all ___ are provided.
Nature reserves such as wildlife sanctuary, national park and ___ help endangered species.
Continuous monitoring and maintenance of effective ___ are part of active management of nature reserves.
___ methods are the ways to conserve animals and plants outside their natural habitats.
Ex situ conservation methods are usually used as a ___.
The three methods of ex situ conservation are ___ of animals, storage of seeds in the seed bank and cultivation of plants in botanical garden.
In captive breeding programmes, individuals of endangered species are bred in ___ and parks.
___ (eggs, sperm cells and embryos can be frozen for future use) is a technique used in captive breeding.
Keeping a ___ prevents from inbreeding in captive breeding.
It has been estimated that ___% of the world’s flowering plant species could disappear within the next 50 years.
Seeds in a seed bank are kept in cold, dark conditions in which the ___ of the seed slows down.
Seeds are dried, then stored in a temperature of ___ $ \\circ C$ and in dark conditions.
Seed banks protect and save plant ___ diversity.
About ___ plant species are grown in botanical gardens all over the world.
Botanical gardens serve as ___ and systematic research centers.
One of the major objectives of botanical gardens is to create and support collections of ___ taxa.
In Myanmar, ___ protected areas have been established.
___ reserves are held for protection of features for ‘scientific study, research, education, and the benefit of the country’.
No settlement or ___ is allowed in a national park.
A ___ is a permanent marine reservation for the conservation of species.
___ is a protected area of importance for flora, fauna, or features of geological or other special interest.
In a ___, capturing, killing and poaching of animals is strictly prohibited.
In the early 1990s, the Government of Myanmar introduced ___ as a way to protect endangered forest resources.
Community forest means all ___ management and utilization activities in which the local community itself is involved.
